hero.subtitle2: The factory model for AI-assisted development.
hero.social: Open source · MIT License · Works offline

problem.label: 01 / the problem
problem.title: AI agents are capable but undisciplined.
problem.card1Desc: They ship code that looks correct but breaks in production.
problem.card2Desc: They overwrite each other's work without noticing.
problem.card3Desc: They generate code fast but produce hidden OpEx: hallucinated dependencies, superficial error handling, and subtle correctness gaps.
problem.metr: When developers used AI, they took 19% longer. Developers estimated AI saved them 20% of the time. Experts predicted 38% faster.
problem.conclusion: The gap between "capable" and "reliable" is not intelligence. It's the harness.

howitworks.label: 02 / how it works
howitworks.title: Six phases. Every time.
howitworks.subtitle: Every project follows the same disciplined flow. No shortcuts. No skipped steps.
howitworks.phase1Desc: Write the spec before any code. Interview requirements. Lock scope.
howitworks.phase2Desc: Break work into atomic tasks. Estimate effort. Identify dependencies.
howitworks.phase3Desc: Implement one slice at a time. Test each before expanding.
howitworks.phase4Desc: Run tests. Check builds. Verify behavior matches spec.
howitworks.phase5Desc: Code review. Security audit. Performance check. Quality gates.
howitworks.conclusion: No phase is optional. Every step has a skill. Every skill has a gate.

enforcement.label: 03 / the harness
enforcement.title: Rules that depend on memory fail.
enforcement.titleAccent: Rules that depend on the harness succeed.
enforcement.level1Desc: Who the agent is, what it cares about, what it must never do. AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, STEERING-GUIDE.md.
enforcement.level2Desc: Task-specific capabilities loaded on demand. 55 skills, 47 guides, eval system, terminal, CI.
enforcement.level3Desc: When tools fire, how agents coordinate, and deterministic hooks at lifecycle points. Pre-commit v8 (9 gates), commit-msg v5, commit-approval.sh.
enforcement.incidentRef: It broke its own rules three times in two days. Then it built a harness that made breaking them impossible.
enforcement.conclusion: It learns from its own failures. Faster than competitors ship features.
enforcement.level4Desc: Evidence it's working or quietly drifting. project-metrics, HEALTH-CHECK.md, validate-skill-table.sh, PROGRESS_STATUS.md.

philosophy.label: 04 / philosophy
philosophy.principle1: Quality emerges from explicit contracts, not implicit trust.
philosophy.principle2: Rules that depend on memory fail. Rules that depend on visible blocks succeed.
philosophy.principle3: A hook that accepts any token is a suggestion. A hook that verifies integrity is a gate.
philosophy.principle4: Speed is a side effect of precision, never the primary goal.
philosophy.principle5: Context is finite. Evict before you drown.
philosophy.principle6: "No, that's overcomplicated" is more valuable than blind compliance.
philosophy.principle7: Learns from its own failures faster than competitors ship features.
philosophy.principle8: Generation is solved. Verification, judgment, and direction are the new craft.
philosophy.principle9: Agent = Model + Harness. Most agent failures are configuration failures.
philosophy.compact: Turns AI coding agents from obedient servants into disciplined senior engineers.

compatible.label: 05 / compatible agents
compatible.title: Designed for OpenCode.
compatible.subtitle: One install. Any agent. Any stack.
compatible.stackDesc: Works with Node, Rust, Python, Go, Ruby, Dart, or any stack.
compatible.stackDetail: init-agents detects your stack automatically. Creates STACK_CONFIG.md.

skills.label: 06 / skills
skills.title: Everything you need.
skills.stats: 55 skills · 47 guides · 6 harness components · eval ready

quickstart.label: 07 / quick start
quickstart.title: Start in seconds.
quickstart.conclusion: Your agent now has 55 skills + 47 guides + 6 harness components. Every project. Every time.

faq.label: 08 / faq
faq.title: Questions.
faq.a1: A framework of 55 composable skills that turn AI coding agents into disciplined senior engineers. It adds mechanical enforcement, not just prompts. Skills follow a 6-phase lifecycle: Define, Plan, Build, Verify, Review, Ship.
faq.q2: How is it different from other skill frameworks?
faq.a2: Three things: (1) Mechanical enforcement via time-window approval and 9 pre-commit gates. (2) The Guardian Pattern: DECISION POINT before every mutation. (3) Context engineering: 258 lines always-loaded (1.9% of 200K context). Other frameworks give you prompts. This gives you process.
faq.a3: Designed for OpenCode. Portable to Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Kiro, Gemini CLI, Devin Desktop, and any agent that reads AGENTS.md. Stack-agnostic: works with Node, Rust, Python, Go, Ruby, Dart, or any stack.
faq.a4: Yes. MIT License. Open source. No subscriptions. No paid tiers. The installer is free, the skills are free, the enforcement is free.
faq.a5: Three levels: (1) Process rules: agent remembers. (2) Visible manifest: agent presents a decision point. (3) Time-window approval: commit-msg hook v5 verifies freshness (<5 min). No tokens, no hash, no friction.
faq.a6: Yes. Skills are Markdown files in the `skills/` directory. Edit them, add new ones, remove ones you don't need. The framework is modular: use all 55 or pick the ones that matter to your workflow.
faq.q7: Can an agent still break the rules despite enforcement?
faq.a7: Yes. The hooks enforce git operations (commit, push) — they require tokens, and tokens require manifests. But no system can fully prevent an agent from acting against protocol. The agent could generate a token without presenting the manifest, interpret ambiguous responses as approval, or push without asking. The human must stay alert. These gates create friction, not guarantees.
faq.a8: The Harness is everything around the AI model that turns raw intelligence into reliable output. Six components: Instructions & Rules (who the agent is), Tools (what it can do), Sandboxes (where it runs), Orchestration (when things fire), Guardrails & Hooks (what it cannot bypass), and Observability (evidence it works). This project is a complete open-source Harness. See docs/HARNESS.md.
faq.a9: The Factory Model means the developer's primary output is not code — it's the system that produces code. You design the harness: specifications, agents, tests, quality gates, feedback loops. The agents handle implementation. You handle architecture, verification, and judgment. Your output is the factory, not the widgets.
faq.a10: The concepts are based on 'The New SDLC With Vibe Coding' by Addy Osmani, Shubham Saboo, and Sokratis Kartakis (May 2026). The paper defines the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the Harness (Agent = Model + Harness), and the Factory Model. This project is an open-source implementation of those principles.
